proposed_from.topic: Minimax estimation rate for the ATE under structure-agnostic nuisance estimation when overlap decays one-sidedly near the propensity-score boundary. Focal object: the minimax rate threshold as a joint function of the overlap-decay exponent and nuisance smoothness, characterizing the regime where one-sided overlap decay strictly changes the achievable ATE rate relative to the bounded-overlap structure-agnostic result.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis": "The closest in-repo comparators are archived stat_sa_cate_pointwise/v1, which studies bounded-overlap pointwise CATE rates and explicitly excluded weak-overlap sequences; downgraded eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier/v1, whose reviews demanded primitive empirical-process and minimax-converse channels rather than assumed frontier activity; and failed pid_overlap_tail_phase/v1, which treated overlap tails as a partial-identification-width question rather than an estimation-rate theorem. The present proposal stays in the Stat cluster and narrows the missing channel to the original full-population ATE under one-sided propensity-tail decay.", "published_axis": "The closest published comparators are JinSyrgkanis2024StructureAgnosticDR and BonviniKennedyDukesBalakrishnan2024StructureAgnosticATE for regular-overlap structure-agnostic ATE optimality, Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats for weak-overlap thresholded AIPW inference, KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification for inverse-weight irregularity, and KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE for lower-bound machinery. None of these papers states a matched one-sided weak-overlap ATE minimax frontier with the explicit phase object rho_n(kappa,beta), a boundary-completed upper construction, and tail-localized least-favorable pairs."}
seed_list: BonviniKennedyDukesBalakrishnan2024StructureAgnosticATE and Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats leave open a one-sided weak-overlap ATE minimax phase diagram; seed: a clipped boundary-completed AIPW estimator with rate rho_n(kappa,beta) and a matching converse.

ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML and ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RegularizedRiesz leave weak-overlap Riesz divergence at a high level; seed: derive the primitive empirical-process and lower-packing frontier for tail-diverging ATE representers.

HongLeungLi2020FinitePopulationLimitedOverlap and HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity aggregate extreme propensities; seed: separate ATE, ATT, and CATT minimax rates under one-sided and two-sided tail decay.
KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE gives localized lower-bound machinery; seed: move the localization from a fixed covariate point to the propensity boundary for global ATE.
MaSantAnnaSasakiUra2023DRWeakOverlap and Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats use thresholding under weak overlap; seed: construct an adaptive clipped AIPW estimator over unknown kappa and beta.

RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanDerVaart2017MinimaxHOIF and BonviniKennedyDukesBalakrishnan2024StructureAgnosticATE suggest higher-order gains; seed: characterize when tail-weighted HOIF beats first-order clipped AIPW.

KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification and HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity suggest a boundary at finite inverse-weight variance; seed: prove the exact kappa = 1 logarithmic transition for ATE rates and inference.
MaWang2020RobustIPW suggests trimming-robust inference; seed: prove an impossibility theorem for uniformly adaptive confidence intervals across kappa-neighborhoods crossing 1.
proposed_from.last_draft_handoff: {"status":"completed","message":"Patched the flagged items in the proposal core: separated the bounded-overlap comparator onto the regular-overlap class W_n^reg/E_n^reg, replaced the malformed least-favorable propensity perturbation with a single clipped expit-logit map, and added Armstrong-Kolesar to the prose positioning. The revised core was written to the requested path and the JSON parses with jq.","artifacts":["/<repo-root>/CausalSmith/doc/formalization/research/stat_ate_overlap_decay/discovery/stat_ate_overlap_decay_proto_core.json"],"literature_checklist":[{"author":"Jin and Syrgkanis","year":2024,"venue":"arXiv preprint","bibkey":"JinSyrgkanis2024StructureAgnosticDR","one_line":"Bounded-overlap structure-agnostic ATE benchmark used as the explicit regular-overlap comparator for the weak-tail frontier.","relevant_to":"prop:strict-overlap-sanity"},{"author":"Bonvini, Kennedy, Dukes, and Balakrishnan","year":2024,"venue":"arXiv preprint","bibkey":"BonviniKennedyDukesBalakrishnan2024StructureAgnosticATE","one_line":"Smoothness-aware bounded-overlap ATE minimax comparator used to position the kappa-shell frontier as an overlap-axis extension rather than a new estimand.","relevant_to":"conj:minimax-frontier"},{"author":"Armstrong and Kolesar","year":2021,"venue":"Econometrica","bibkey":"ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE","one_line":"Added to related_work after opening the paper: it handles lack of overlap through fixed-design finite-sample optimality over explicit smoothness classes, not a random-design structure-agnostic kappa-shell minimax frontier.","relevant_to":"prop:comparator-separation"},{"author":"Dorn","year":2025,"venue":"arXiv preprint","bibkey":"Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats","one_line":"Weak-overlap thresholded doubly robust inference comparator for the upper-envelope side; still not a matched minimax frontier.","relevant_to":"thm:clipped-upper"},{"author":"Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman","year":2024,"venue":"Annals of Statistics","bibkey":"KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE","one_line":"Localized fuzzy-hypotheses lower-bound template informing the propensity-boundary converse handle.","relevant_to":"conj:tail-lower-bound"},{"author":"Khan and Tamer","year":2010,"venue":"Econometrica","bibkey":"KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification","one_line":"Irregular inverse-weight tail benchmark used for the tail-order reduction and the motivation for rate deterioration under weak overlap.","relevant_to":"prop:tail-order-reduction"}]}
proposed_from.literature_map:
Hirano, Imbens, and Ridder (2003) give the classical bounded-overlap semiparametric ATE benchmark.
Crump, Hotz, Imbens, and Mitnik (2009) address poor overlap by changing the target population rather than preserving the full ATE.
Khan and Tamer (2010) are the core irregularity anchor: inverse-weight treatment-effect estimation slows when support/tail conditions deteriorate.
Hong, Leung, and Li (2020) and Heiler and Kazak (2021) show slower or non-Gaussian behavior under limited overlap and many extreme propensities, but not a kappa-beta ATE minimax frontier.
Ma and Wang (2020) and Ma, Sant'Anna, Sasaki, and Ura (2023) develop trimming-robust weak-overlap inference while preserving or changing targets, without a sharp minimax phase diagram.
Chernozhukov et al. (2018) provide the orthogonal-score and cross-fitting expansion that the upper-bound side should adapt.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022a, 2022b) frame Riesz-based debiasing and regularized representer estimation, but not the primitive weak-overlap frontier when the ATE representer diverges at the propensity boundary.
Jin and Syrgkanis (2024) and Bonvini, Kennedy, Dukes, and Balakrishnan (2024) are the closest regular-overlap structure-agnostic comparators; both leave the one-sided weak-overlap benchmark open.
Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, and Wasserman (2024) provide the lower-bound template to adapt, with localization moved from x-space to the propensity boundary.
Dorn (2025) is the closest estimator-level weak-overlap paper; it proves thresholded AIPW inference conditions, not a matched minimax frontier.
The in-repo predecessors stat_sa_cate_pointwise and eid_dml_orthogonality_frontier supply the two relevant open channels: a lower-bound template that excluded weak-overlap sequences, and a demand for primitive empirical-process and converse arguments rather than assumed frontier activity.
The chosen kernel is therefore the weakest novel synthesis that still closes a real gap: a full-ATE one-sided weak-overlap rate frontier with an explicit threshold in kappa and beta.

seed_details.one_line: BonviniKennedyDukesBalakrishnan2024StructureAgnosticATE and Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats leave open a one-sided weak-overlap ATE minimax phase diagram; seed: a clipped boundary-completed AIPW estimator with rate rho_n(kappa,beta) and a matching converse.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Dorn because thresholded AIPW already handles weak overlap.
seed_details.rebuttal: Dorn gives sufficient inference conditions, not a matched minimax upper/lower rate frontier over one-sided tail decay and boundary smoothness.

seed_details.one_line: ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML and ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022RegularizedRiesz leave weak-overlap Riesz divergence at a high level; seed: derive the primitive empirical-process and lower-packing frontier for tail-diverging ATE representers.
seed_details.referee_objection: Regularized Riesz bounds already imply the answer because ATE is a linear functional.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those papers do not derive the tail-local norm growth or the lower-bound witness family when the representer diverges near e(X)=0.

seed_details.one_line: HongLeungLi2020FinitePopulationLimitedOverlap and HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity aggregate extreme propensities; seed: separate ATE, ATT, and CATT minimax rates under one-sided and two-sided tail decay.
seed_details.referee_objection: ATT follows by a change of measure, so the result is definitional.
seed_details.rebuttal: The ATT one-tail heuristic is only part of the story; the publishable content is the joint class-level minimax separation across estimands with matched lower witnesses.

seed_details.one_line: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE gives localized lower-bound machinery; seed: move the localization from a fixed covariate point to the propensity boundary for global ATE.
seed_details.referee_objection: Apply the CATE lower bound at a boundary point and you are done.
seed_details.rebuttal: ATE under weak overlap is an integrated boundary problem with inverse-weight rarity, not a pointwise CATE problem.

seed_details.one_line: MaSantAnnaSasakiUra2023DRWeakOverlap and Dorn2025WeakOverlapDRTStats use thresholding under weak overlap; seed: construct an adaptive clipped AIPW estimator over unknown kappa and beta.
seed_details.referee_objection: Threshold selection is already handled by weak-overlap practice rules.
seed_details.rebuttal: Practice rules are not oracle inequalities over a continuum of tail and smoothness classes.

seed_details.one_line: RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanDerVaart2017MinimaxHOIF and BonviniKennedyDukesBalakrishnan2024StructureAgnosticATE suggest higher-order gains; seed: characterize when tail-weighted HOIF beats first-order clipped AIPW.
seed_details.referee_objection: HOIF optimality already covers low-regularity functionals.
seed_details.rebuttal: The unresolved issue is whether higher-order gains survive the variance blow-up induced by one-sided weak overlap.

seed_details.one_line: KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification and HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity suggest a boundary at finite inverse-weight variance; seed: prove the exact kappa = 1 logarithmic transition for ATE rates and inference.
seed_details.referee_objection: The moment check E[1/e(X)]<infinity already tells you the transition.
seed_details.rebuttal: A moment boundary is not yet a matched minimax rate and lower-bound statement with the correct logarithmic term.

seed_details.one_line: MaWang2020RobustIPW suggests trimming-robust inference; seed: prove an impossibility theorem for uniformly adaptive confidence intervals across kappa-neighborhoods crossing 1.
seed_details.referee_objection: Robust trimming inference already addresses this problem.
seed_details.rebuttal: Robustness to trimming choices is not uniform minimax adaptivity across a phase boundary in kappa.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:tail-lower-bound / conj:minimax-frontier
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The Le Cam / Assouad lower-bound family is an open solve target, not an assumed class condition.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The minimax frontier, phase diagram, and strict-deterioration claim are accepted only conditional on proving the stated bounded-divergence converse.

banked_reason: Stage 0.5 decision FAIL — headline sharp weak-overlap minimax frontier (conj:minimax-frontier) underdelivered: the class-uniform U_n≍R_n comparison and R_n^* upper frontier are not discharged (scope_overreach_on_rate_premise + hidden_fold_structure + nonuniform_comparison_gap), leaving a parametric lower floor plus estimator-side O_P(U_n) achievability; crossfit-estimator / tail-order-reduction kernel is reusable (solver-blocked).
